perturbation.original_text: Without the prior written consent of CMC (which may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion), for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, neither Valco nor Brubaker shall, directly or indirectly, whether as a stockholder, individual, partner, agent, representative, employee, employer, director, officer, principal, consultant, advisor, or independent contractor, or through any of the foregoing, or in any other relation or capacity whatsoever: (I) engage in the business relating to sand and gravel mining or sales of ready mix concrete, asphalt and construction aggregates, in Pueblo and/or El Paso Counties, Colorado; (ii) operate or own a concrete batch plant, aggregates operation or asphalt plant in Teller County, Colorado; or (iii) except to Valco's present customers and prospective customers in similar businesses, make any sales to any customers in Teller County, Colorado.
perturbation.changed_text: Without the prior written consent of CMC (which may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion), for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, neither Valco nor Brubaker shall, directly or indirectly, whether as a stockholder, individual, partner, agent, representative, employee, employer, director, officer, principal, consultant, advisor, or independent contractor, or through any of the foregoing, or in any other relation or capacity whatsoever: (I) engage in the business relating to sand and gravel mining or sales of ready mix concrete, asphalt and construction aggregates, in Pueblo County, Colorado; (ii) operate or own a concrete batch plant, aggregates operation or asphalt plant in Teller County, Colorado; or (iii) except to Valco's present customers and prospective customers in similar businesses, make any sales to any customers in Teller County, Colorado.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and/or El Paso Counties, Colorado' from the prohibited regions for sand and gravel mining, an ambiguity is introduced. Later in section 2, under (b) The contract specifies non-solicitation of employees only for Valco's current or former Pueblo area employees. This contrasts with the earlier definition of the prohibited region for sand and gravel mining, which previously included both Pueblo and El Paso Counties. This creates a contradiction concerning whether Valco and Brubaker are restricted from operating in El Paso County or soliciting employees from El Paso County.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Without the prior written consent of CMC (which may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion), for a period of two (2) years from and after the date hereof, neither Valco nor Brubaker shall, directly or indirectly, whether as a stockholder, individual, partner, agent, representative, employee, employer, director, officer, principal, consultant, advisor, or independent contractor, or through any of the foregoing, or in any other relation or capacity whatsoever, solicit employment of any of Valco's current or former Pueblo area employees who are retained by CMC or any of its subsidiaries in connection with the Pueblo operations, or encourage any such employees to leave the employ of CMC or any of its subsidiaries.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited certain business activities in both Pueblo and El Paso Counties. The changed text removes 'and/or El Paso Counties, Colorado', thereby narrowing the prohibited region to only Pueblo County for sand and gravel mining. The contradicted text, section (b), refers to 'Pueblo area employees'. This creates a contradiction: the non-solicitation clause applies only to Pueblo area employees, but the original restriction on business activities extended to El Paso County. By removing El Paso County from the initial restriction, it creates ambiguity and a potential contradiction as to whether Valco and Brubaker are now permitted to solicit employees who work in El Paso County, as the non-solicitation clause only refers to Pueblo employees and the changed text only restricts business activity in Pueblo County. This satisfies the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction', where key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: Without the prior written consent of CMC (which may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion), for a period of two (2) years from and after the date hereof, neither Valco nor Brubaker shall, directly or indirectly, whether as a stockholder, individual, partner, agent, representative, employee, employer, director, officer, principal, consultant, advisor, or independent contractor, or through any of the foregoing, or in any other relation or capacity whatsoever, solicit employment of any of Valco's current or former Pueblo area employees who are retained by CMC or any of its subsidiaries in connection with the Pueblo operations, or encourage any such employees to leave the employ of CMC or any of its subsidiaries.
perturbation.changed_text: Without the prior written consent of CMC (which may be withheld in CMC's reasonable discretion), for a period of two (2) years from and after the date hereof, neither Valco nor Brubaker shall, directly or indirectly, whether as a stockholder, individual, partner, agent, representative, employee, employer, director, officer, principal, consultant, advisor, or independent contractor, or through any of the foregoing, or in any other relation or capacity whatsoever, solicit employment of any of Valco's current or former Pueblo area employees who are retained by CMC or any of its subsidiaries in connection with the Pueblo operations, or encourage any such employees to leave the employ of CMC or any of its subsidiaries.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'sole discretion' to 'reasonable discretion' regarding CMC's consent, an ambiguity is created as section 2(a) states the prior written consent 'may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion'. This change creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether CMC has total freedom to withhold consent, as suggested by 'sole discretion' in 2(a), or if that freedom is limited by a reasonableness standard, as the edited version of 2(b) implies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Without the prior written consent of CMC (which may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion), for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, neither Valco nor Brubaker shall, directly or indirectly, whether as a stockholder, individual, partner, agent, representative, employee, employer, director, officer, principal, consultant, advisor, or independent contractor, or through any of the foregoing, or in any other relation or capacity whatsoever: (I) engage in the business relating to sand and gravel mining or sales of ready mix concrete, asphalt and construction aggregates, in Pueblo County, Colorado; (ii) operate or own a concrete batch plant, aggregates operation or asphalt plant in Teller County, Colorado; or (iii) except to Valco's present customers and prospective customers in similar businesses, make any sales to any customers in Teller County, Colorado.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'sole discretion' to 'reasonable discretion'. Section (a) in the 'Contradicted Text' explicitly states that consent 'may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion'. The modification in the changed text directly contradicts this by implying that CMC's discretion is now subject to a reasonableness standard, creating an in-text contradiction as described in the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: (a) Except as provided in Subsection (b) below, each of Valco and Brubaker agrees that, for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, all information previously or hereafter disclosed to any of them by CMC in connection with the transactions contemplated by the Acquisition Agreement and Lease and information relating to Valco's (after the date hereof CMC's) Pueblo operations is confidential (collectively, "Confidential Information") and shall be held in strict confidence and not disclosed to any person or entity.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) Except as provided in Subsection (b) below, each of Valco and Brubaker agrees that, for a period of five (5) years from and after the date hereof, all information previously or hereafter disclosed to any of them by CMC in connection with the transactions contemplated by the Acquisition Agreement and Lease and information relating to Valco's (after the date hereof CMC's) Pueblo operations is confidential (collectively, "Confidential Information") and shall be held in strict confidence and not disclosed to any person or entity.
perturbation.explanation: The change of the confidentiality period from ten (10) years to five (5) creates an internal contradiction. Later in section 4(a) it refers to the obligations of Valco and Brubaker. This contradicts the initial confidentiality term defined in section 3, where it now states 'five years', therefore is creating an uncertainty when should be considered a breach for disclosing information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Valco's and Brubaker's obligations hereunder shall be joint and several as long as Brubaker controls Valco. If Brubaker no longer controls Valco, Valco's and Brubaker's obligation herein shall be several. Each of Valco and Brubaker recognizes that it would be impossible to measure in money all the damages which will accrue to CMC by reason of a failure to comply with the restrictions and perform the obligations under this Agreement. Each of Valco and Brubaker hereby acknowledges that CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law and CMC shall, in addition to and not in lieu of money damages, be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief against Valco and Brubaker in an action or procedure to enforce the provisions hereof. Valco and Brubaker shall reimburse CMC for its expenses, including reasonable attorney's fees, incurred in connection with the enforcement of the provisions hereof relating to a breach of this Agreement by Valco or Brubaker.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a confidentiality period of ten (10) years, while the changed text reduces it to five (5) years. The contradicted text references Valco's and Brubaker's obligations under the agreement, and these obligations now have an uncertain timeframe due to the change in the confidentiality period, therefore the change from ten years to five years, creates a direct conflict in the term of obligation, hence contradicting the contradicted text as it will not be clear how long are Valco's and Brubaker's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Valco and Brubaker shall have no requirement to keep information confidential, and no such information shall be considered Confidential Information, to the extent any of the following applies: (I) the information was within the public domain at the time it was first known or provided to Valco and Brubaker; (ii) the information was published or otherwise became part of the public domain after it was first known or provided to Valco and Brubaker through no fault of either of them or their respective directors, officers, agents employees or affiliates; or (iii) the information is required to be disclosed (x) by any federal or state law, rule or regulation, (y) by any applicable judgment, order or decree of any court, governmental agency or arbitrator having or purporting to have jurisdiction in the matter, or (z) pursuant to any subpoena or other discovery request in any litigation, arbitration or other proceeding; provided, however, that if any of Valco and Brubaker proposes to disclose the information in accordance with (x), (y) or (z), such party shall, to the extent feasible, first give CMC reasonable prior notice of the proposed disclosure of any such information to the application of such law, rule or regulation, or to appear before any court, governmental agency or arbitration order to contest the disclosure, as the case may be.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Valco and Brubaker shall have no requirement to keep information confidential, and no such information shall be considered Confidential Information, to the extent any of the following applies: (I) the information was within the public domain at the time it was first known or provided to Valco and Brubaker; (ii) the information was published or otherwise became part of the public domain after it was first known or provided to Valco and Brubaker; or (iii) the information is required to be disclosed (x) by any federal or state law, rule or regulation, (y) by any applicable judgment, order or decree of any court, governmental agency or arbitrator having or purporting to have jurisdiction in the matter, or (z) pursuant to any subpoena or other discovery request in any litigation, arbitration or other proceeding; provided, however, that if any of Valco and Brubaker proposes to disclose the information in accordance with (x), (y) or (z), such party must give CMC immediate prior notice of the proposed disclosure of any such information to the application of such law, rule or regulation, or to appear before any court, governmental agency or arbitration order to contest the disclosure, as the case may be.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'to the extent feasible, first give CMC reasonable prior notice' to 'must give CMC immediate prior notice' creates an internal contradiction with the overall confidentiality and non-disclosure terms. Section 3(a) establishes that confidential information must be held in 'strict confidence and not disclosed'. Then this alteration forces an 'immediate' notification, the obligation of keeping strict confidence with the confidential information could be broken if the notification its immediate and not with 'reasonable prior notice'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Except as provided in Subsection (b) below, each of Valco and Brubaker agrees that, for a period of five (5) years from and after the date hereof, all information previously or hereafter disclosed to any of them by CMC in connection with the transactions contemplated by the Acquisition Agreement and Lease and information relating to Valco's (after the date hereof CMC's) Pueblo operations is confidential (collectively, "Confidential Information") and shall be held in strict confidence and not disclosed to any person or entity.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for "reasonable prior notice" before disclosing confidential information. The changed text mandates "immediate prior notice". This directly contradicts section (a), which requires the information to be held in "strict confidence and not disclosed". Immediate disclosure could reasonably be interpreted as violating the 'strict confidence' requirement. This is because, despite the provision allowing for disclosure under certain legal obligations, the original text provided a buffer to maintain confidentiality as long as feasible by giving reasonable prior notice, whereas the changed text removes that and creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: With CMC's prior written consent (which will not be unreasonably withheld), Valco and Brubaker may disclose, on a need to know basis, Confidential Information to consultants, advisors and institutional lenders, subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c). Valco also may disclose, on a need to know basis, and subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c), to any bona fide acquirer (whether by purchase, exchange, merger or otherwise) of the stock, of substantially all of the assets of Valco, or of the interest of Valco under the Lease, the formula under the Lease for determining the Production Royalty Rate, the historical revenues received under the Lease, the total tonnage mined (on an aggregate and not product type basis) under the Lease and total remaining tons to be mined under the Lease, and with CMC's prior written consent (not 2 to be unreasonably withheld), any other information concerning the Lease, but CMC may withhold such consent in its sole discretion as to such other information if in CMC's sole judgment a recipient of such other information is or could be expected to become a competitor of CMC in the Pueblo area. In the case of any permitted disclosure of Confidential Information under this paragraph (c), Valco and Brubaker shall inform such persons of the existence of this Agreement and take all reasonable steps to ensure that such persons comply with the provisions of this Agreement applicable to Valco and Brubaker.
perturbation.changed_text: With CMC's prior written consent (which will not be unreasonably withheld), Valco and Brubaker may disclose, on a need to know basis, Confidential Information to consultants, advisors and institutional lenders, subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c). Valco also may disclose, on a need to know basis, and subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c), to any acquirer (whether by purchase, exchange, merger or otherwise) of the stock, of substantially all of the assets of Valco, or of the interest of Valco under the Lease, the formula under the Lease for determining the Production Royalty Rate, the historical revenues received under the Lease, the total tonnage mined (on an aggregate and not product type basis) under the Lease and total remaining tons to be mined under the Lease, and with CMC's prior written consent (not 2 to be unreasonably withheld), any other information concerning the Lease, but CMC may withhold such consent in its sole discretion as to such other information if in CMC's sole judgment a recipient of such other information is or could be expected to become a competitor of CMC in the Pueblo area. In the case of any permitted disclosure of Confidential Information under this paragraph (c), Valco and Brubaker shall inform such persons of the existence of this Agreement and take all reasonable steps to ensure that such persons comply with the provisions of this Agreement applicable to Valco and Brubaker.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'bona fide' before 'acquirer' creates an ambiguity. This contrasts with the statement 'CMC may withhold such consent in its sole discretion as to such other information if in CMC's sole judgment a recipient of such other information is or could be expected to become a competitor of CMC in the Pueblo area', as it is not clear if it applies to acquirers in general or just 'bona fide acquirers' creating a contradiction and uncertainty about whether CMC has the right to withhold consent from a not bona fide acquirer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: With CMC's prior written consent (which will not be unreasonably withheld), Valco and Brubaker may disclose, on a need to know basis, Confidential Information to consultants, advisors and institutional lenders, subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c). Valco also may disclose, on a need to know basis, and subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c), to any acquirer (whether by purchase, exchange, merger or otherwise) of the stock, of substantially all of the assets of Valco, or of the interest of Valco under the Lease, the formula under the Lease for determining the Production Royalty Rate, the historical revenues received under the Lease, the total tonnage mined (on an aggregate and not product type basis) under the Lease and total remaining tons to be mined under the Lease, and with CMC's prior written consent (not 2 to be unreasonably withheld), any other information concerning the Lease, but CMC may withhold such consent in its sole discretion as to such other information if in CMC's sole judgment a recipient of such other information is or could be expected to become a competitor of CMC in the Pueblo area. In the case of any permitted disclosure of Confidential Information under this paragraph (c), Valco and Brubaker shall inform such persons of the existence of this Agreement and take all reasonable steps to ensure that such persons comply with the provisions of this Agreement applicable to Valco and Brubaker.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies "bona fide acquirer," implying a specific type of acquirer. The changed text removes "bona fide," broadening the definition to include any acquirer. This directly contradicts the clause that allows CMC to withhold consent if a recipient is a potential competitor. The original text suggests that CMC's discretion applies only to 'bona fide acquirers'. The modified text now allows *any* acquirer to obtain information unless CMC objects. This creates an ambiguity and thus a contradiction because it's unclear if CMC can withhold consent from an acquirer that is *not* bona fide, creating uncertainty and a direct conflict of terms within the contract, thereby qualifying it as a strong, impactful contradiction that would cause confusion or a legal issue.

perturbation.original_text: No waiver or amendment to this Agreement shall be valid unless signed in writing by each of Valco, Brubaker and CMC.
perturbation.changed_text: No waiver or amendment to this Agreement shall be valid unless signed in writing by Valco and CMC.
perturbation.explanation: Removing Brubaker from the entities required to sign a waiver or amendment creates ambiguity. Section 4(a) stipulates 'Valco's and Brubaker's obligations hereunder shall be joint and several as long as Brubaker controls Valco'. Thus, altering the waiver/amendment condition creates an in-text contradiction, is uncertain the obligations Brubaker has with the contract due to not being required to sign.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Valco's and Brubaker's obligations hereunder shall be joint and several as long as Brubaker controls Valco. If Brubaker no longer controls Valco, Valco's and Brubaker's obligation herein shall be several. Each of Valco and Brubaker recognizes that it would be impossible to measure in money all the damages which will accrue to CMC by reason of a failure to comply with the restrictions and perform the obligations under this Agreement. Each of Valco and Brubaker hereby acknowledges that CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law and CMC shall, in addition to and not in lieu of money damages, be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief against Valco and Brubaker in an action or procedure to enforce the provisions hereof. Valco and Brubaker shall reimburse CMC for its expenses, including reasonable attorney's fees, incurred in connection with the enforcement of the provisions hereof relating to a breach of this Agreement by Valco or Brubaker.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires Valco, Brubaker, and CMC to sign waivers or amendments. The changed text removes Brubaker from this requirement. However, the contradicted text states that 'Valco's and Brubaker's obligations hereunder shall be joint and several as long as Brubaker controls Valco.' By removing Brubaker's signature requirement, the altered text introduces a conflict because Brubaker still has joint and several obligations as indicated in the contradicted text, which creates an inconsistency, therefore it adheres to the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
